scenario: db-architect agent created RPC get_processor_self_sufficiency without GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION ... TO authenticated. All 13 other migrations in the project include explicit grants. The omission was caught by code-reviewer agent, not by the implementer. Without the grant, the RPC would fail with permission denied when called from the frontend via PostgREST.
expected_findings: Agent instructions do not enforce a mandatory post-migration checklist that includes GRANT verification
Pattern recognition failed: 13/13 prior migrations had explicit GRANT EXECUTE, but the agent did not self-check against this convention
No grep-based verification step to confirm new functions have matching GRANT statements
expected_patch_focus: Add explicit post-migration verification step: grep for GRANT EXECUTE matching every CREATE FUNCTION in the migration
Add convention callout: 'Every RPC function MUST include GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION ... TO authenticated'
Reference the historical pattern: all migrations in supabase/migrations/ include explicit grants

replay_plan.failed_slice: Create a new RPC function migration — verify the agent includes GRANT EXECUTE without prompting
replay_plan.near_neighbor: Create a migration with multiple functions — verify each gets its own GRANT
replay_plan.canary: Apply a simple ALTER TABLE migration with no functions — should not trigger GRANT checks
